articles.description: World shares advanced on Friday, tracking big Wall Street gains, while oil prices slipped after US President Donald Trump claimed there was a breakthrough in talks to end the Iran war.

articles.title: European Markets Surge Amid Oil Price Drop and Diplomatic Hopes
articles.description: European shares rose over 1% as oil prices slipped, driven by hopes of diplomatic progress in the Middle East. The STOXX 600 index gained 1.2%, boosted by gains in travel, leisure, and banking stocks. Meanwhile, German inflation slightly eased, and the UK economy contracted by 0.1% in April.

articles.title: Govt Bars Bulk Industrial Petrol, Diesel Purchases Through Petrol Pumps
articles.description: The differential arose as state-owned oil companies modulated retail prices to insulate common users from the spike in cost that followed the West Asia crisis in late February.

articles.title: Government's new petrol, diesel order: Who is affected, and why was it introduced?
articles.description: The Centre has restricted industrial and commercial bulk fuel purchases from retail pumps. The move aims to protect pump supplies for ordinary consumers amid a wide retail-bulk price gap.

articles.title: World shares surge and oil prices slip after Trump claims a breakthrough in Iran war talks
articles.description: World shares have advanced in the wake of big Wall Street gains, while oil prices slipped more than 1% after U.S. President Donald Trump claimed there was a breakthrough in talks on ending the Iran war. South Korea's Kospi jumped 7.8% and Tokyo's Nikkei 225 gained 3.5%. Trump said he called off military strikes against Iran and had made “a great settlement of the war with Iran.” He said an extension of the war's tenuous ceasefire may be finalized “over the next few days.” On Thursday, the S&P 500 rallied 1.8%. The Dow industrials surged 1.9%, and the Nasdaq composite jumped 2.5%. Investors were awaiting the debut Friday on Wall Street of SpaceX, Elon Musk’s rocket company.
